role: A React region component that renders the right-hand-man's proposal/consent surface, switching between a one-click safe-verb offer (B1) and a select-then-approve comparison for consequential interactive offers (B2).
capabilities: Render proposal options from proposal.options or synthesize a single primary option
B1 one-click approval surface for safe verbs
B2 side-by-side interactive comparison with visual-only selection
Explicit separate approve-and-build commit for interactive offers
Steer/refine text input that sends feedback back to the RHM
Reset local selection and steer text when a new proposal arrives
substance_why: The file fully implements both B1 and B2 consent surfaces, wires all primary and secondary actions, and contains extensive behavioral comments defining the consent model.
interactions_present: Click an option to immediately approveProposal (B1)
Click a comparison card to select/deselect visually (B2)
Click explicit approve & build button to approve selected option (B2)
Type in steer input and submit via button or Enter key
Click queue to inbox to deferProposal
interactions_missing: No confirmation step before running approveProposal
No keyboard navigation/selection for comparison cards
No ARIA state for selected card or busy announcements
No error or success feedback surfaced in the UI
No loading state beyond disabled controls
Phone stacking behavior is only implied by CSS class, not verified/adapted here
No handling for stale selection if options mutate without a full proposal change
reuse_why: The two-mode consent logic and action wiring are conceptually sound and should be preserved, but the markup, styling classes, and accessibility need redesign.
decomposition_hints: Split B1 (OfferAffordance) and B2 (InteractiveComparisonAffordance) into separate region components
Extract a reusable SteerInput subcomponent shared by both modes
Extract a reusable ProposalActionBar for defer/set-aside/dismiss
Extract an OptionCard component for both single-option and comparison rendering
